# Scotty's Gardening Lab

> Regenerative agriculture lab growing nutrient-dense food through living soil science and lacto-fermentation in Spring Branch, TX.

## About

Scotty's Gardening Lab is a regenerative agriculture operation in Spring Branch, Texas (Lat: 29.79N, Long: -98.38W). We produce nutrient-dense food through living soil science and lacto-fermentation. Our methodology — the Law of Returns — is a closed-loop carbon cycle where waste becomes compost, compost feeds soil biology, and biology grows food.

## What We Do

- Grow salad greens, radishes, and other produce in living soil with full prokaryotic association
- Raise pasture-raised ducks for nutrient-dense eggs
- Craft lacto-fermented foods (kimchi, escabeche) using natural Lactobacillus bacteria
- Practice no-till, no synthetic fertilizer regenerative agriculture
- Lab-tested crops at up to 300% higher nutrient density vs conventional produce

## Products

- Living Soil Salad Mix — $8.00
- Spicy Radishes (Bunch) — $4.00
- Pasture-Raised Duck Eggs — seasonal availability
- Fermented Kimchi (16oz) — $12.00
- Escabeche — $10.00

## Key Concepts

- **Living Soil**: Contains billions of microorganisms per gram forming symbiotic relationships with plant roots, providing bio-available nutrients through prokaryotic association
- **Law of Returns**: Closed-loop carbon cycle — kitchen scraps, yard waste, and organic matter returned to soil rather than landfill
- **Lacto-fermentation**: Uses naturally occurring Lactobacillus bacteria to convert sugars into lactic acid, preserving food without vinegar or heat processing
- **Prokaryotic Association**: Re-engaging beneficial bacteria in both soil (for growing) and gut (for health)

## Location

Spring Branch, Texas (Central Texas Hill Country, Balcones Canyonlands ecology)
Local pickup only.
